label: Other male genital
quick_take: C63 is a residual category (scrotum, spermatic cord, other). Very small numbers globally — there is no credible trend or level story, only coding-practice signals. Useful mainly as a data-quality flag.

findings.title: Everywhere is near-zero
findings.text: Most registries fall to 0.00 by vol XII. The "biggest decreases" panel is dominated by registries with 0.27 → 0 transitions — more an artefact of rounding than a trend. Previously reported outliers (USA LA-Korean vol V 3.33; France Hérault, Korea Ulsan, Japan Saga rising +400 to +800 %) all sit on 1–15 cases per registry-volume and do not support a primary finding.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Everywhere is near-zero

findings.text: Aggregate category with very small counts. Do NOT publish findings on "other male genital cancer" without descending to actual morphology codes.
Historically, some C63 cases were coded under "unspecified urogenital" depending on registry — trend interpretation is essentially meaningless.
None as a primary story. Useful mainly as a *data-quality* flag — registries with persistently high C63 rates may have coding issues in prostate / testis / penis.
